channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
item.description: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.
item.content:encoded:
The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
item.description: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.
item.content:encoded: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)

item.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
item.description: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
item.description: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.
item.content:encoded:
In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

(Image credit: 朝鮮通信社/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
item.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

(Image credit: Andy Wong/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
item.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.
item.content:encoded:
The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

(Image credit: U.S. Navy)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Denmark holds early elections spurred by Trump's threats to take Greenland
item.description: Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.
item.content:encoded:
Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.

(Image credit: Rob Schmitz)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
item.description: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.
item.content:encoded: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
